title: Classify Parallelogram by Parallel Sides
description: Determine that a quadrilateral is a parallelogram if both pairs of opposite sides are parallel.
condition: A quadrilateral with both pairs of opposite sides parallel (e.g., same direction or slope on a grid).
response: Conclude the quadrilateral is a parallelogram.
label: Concept
example: If \(AB \parallel CD\) and \(AD \parallel BC\), then \(ABCD\) is a parallelogram.

title: Classify Rectangle by Right Angles
description: Identify a quadrilateral as a rectangle if it has four right angles.
condition: A quadrilateral with four right angles.
response: Conclude the quadrilateral is a rectangle.
label: Concept
example: If all four angles measure \(90^\circ\), then the quadrilateral is a rectangle.

title: Classify Rhombus by Equal Sides
description: Identify a quadrilateral as a rhombus if all four sides are equal in length.
condition: A quadrilateral with four equal side lengths.
response: Conclude the quadrilateral is a rhombus.
label: Concept
example: If \(AB = BC = CD = DA\), then the quadrilateral is a rhombus.

title: Determine Always/Sometimes/Never Relationship
description: Decide whether one geometric category is always, sometimes, or never another based on definitions.
condition: A statement comparing two quadrilateral categories (e.g., rectangle and rhombus).
response: Select 'always,' 'sometimes,' or 'never' based on definitions and counterexamples.
label: Principle
example: A rectangle is sometimes a rhombus because a square is both a rectangle and a rhombus.

title: Find Corresponding Value by Doubling
description: Determine the corresponding value in one pattern when it is twice the value of another pattern.
condition: Two corresponding pattern values where one rule adds 2 starting at 0 and the other adds 4 starting at 0 (relationship: second pattern is double the first); given a value from the first pattern.
response: Multiply the given value by 2 to find the corresponding value.
label: Principle / Model
example: If Lin's number is $26$, then Priya's number is $2 \times 26 = 52$.

title: Find Corresponding Value by Halving
description: Determine the corresponding value in one pattern when it is half the value of another pattern.
condition: Two corresponding pattern values where one pattern is double the other; given a value from the larger (double) pattern.
response: Divide the given value by 2 to find the corresponding value.
label: Principle / Model
example: If Priya's number is $240$, then Lin's number is $240 \div 2 = 120$.

title: Identify a Right Triangle
description: Recognize that a triangle with one $90^\circ$ angle is a right triangle.
condition: A triangle has one angle measuring $90^\circ$.
response: Classify the triangle as a right triangle.
label: Concept
example: If triangle $ABC$ has $\angle A = 90^\circ$, then it is a right triangle.

title: Identify a Trapezoid
description: Recognize that a quadrilateral with at least one pair of parallel sides is a trapezoid.
condition: A quadrilateral has at least one pair of parallel sides.
response: Classify the quadrilateral as a trapezoid.
label: Concept
example: If quadrilateral $ABCD$ has $AB \parallel CD$, then it is a trapezoid.

title: Identify a Triangle with Two Equal Sides
description: Recognize that a triangle with two equal side lengths is isosceles.
condition: A triangle has at least two sides of equal length.
response: Classify the triangle as having 2 equal side lengths (isosceles).
label: Concept
example: If $AB = AC$ in $\triangle ABC$, then it has two equal side lengths (isosceles).

title: Identify Coordinates from Grid
description: Read and state the ordered pair of a plotted point by counting horizontal and vertical distances from the origin.
condition: A point plotted on a coordinate grid.
response: State its coordinates as \((x, y)\) with horizontal value first and vertical value second.
label: Rule
example: If a point is 3 units right and 8 units up, its coordinates are \((3, 8)\).

title: Identify Horizontal Line from Equal y-Coordinates
description: Determine that a set of points lies on a horizontal line when all points share the same y-coordinate.
condition: Given two or more ordered pairs where the y-values are identical and the x-values may differ.
response: State that the points lie on a horizontal line.
label: Principle / Model
example: Points $(1,5), (2,5), (3,5)$ all have $y=5$, so they lie on a horizontal line.

title: Identify Multiplicative Relationship Between Patterns
description: Recognize that corresponding terms in two additive patterns form a multiplicative relationship.
condition: Given two number patterns starting at 0, one increasing by 2 and one increasing by 4, with aligned terms.
response: State that one pattern's terms are twice (or half) the corresponding terms of the other.
label: Concept
example: Comparing $(0,0), (2,4), (4,8)$ shows that each Priya value equals $2 \times$ the Lin value.

title: Identify Neither Horizontal Nor Vertical
description: Determine that a set of points does not lie on a single horizontal or vertical line when neither all x-values nor all y-values are equal.
condition: Given two or more ordered pairs where the x-values are not all equal and the y-values are not all equal.
response: State that the points lie on neither a horizontal nor a vertical line.
label: Principle / Model
example: Points $(1,1), (2,2), (3,3)$ do not share the same $x$ or the same $y$, so they lie on neither a horizontal nor a vertical line.

title: Identify Vertical Line from Equal x-Coordinates
description: Determine that a set of points lies on a vertical line when all points share the same x-coordinate.
condition: Given two or more ordered pairs where the x-values are identical and the y-values may differ.
response: State that the points lie on a vertical line.
label: Principle / Model
example: Points $(4,1), (4,3), (4,8)$ all have $x=4$, so they lie on a vertical line.
notes: Not explicitly used in provided sets but required to evaluate all cases.

title: Interpret Axes as Quantities
description: Interpret the meaning of x- and y-coordinates in context based on axis labels.
condition: A coordinate graph with labeled horizontal and vertical axes representing quantities.
response: Associate the x-coordinate with the horizontal quantity and the y-coordinate with the vertical quantity.
label: Rule
example: If the horizontal axis is 'number of pencils' and the vertical axis is 'number of pens,' then point \((7,3)\) represents 7 pencils and 3 pens.

title: Interpret Coordinate Point in Context
description: Interpret a plotted point on a coordinate grid using axis labels and units.
condition: Given a labeled point on a coordinate grid with x-axis representing age (years) and y-axis representing height (inches).
response: State the ordered pair meaning in context (age, height).
label: Rule / Plan
example: Point $S$ at $(1, 30)$ means the child is 1 year old and 30 inches tall.
notes: Relies on convention that x-coordinate is horizontal axis and y-coordinate is vertical axis.

title: Interpret Ordered Pair as (x, y)
description: Understand that in an ordered pair (x, y), the first value represents horizontal position (x-coordinate) and the second represents vertical position (y-coordinate).
condition: Given an ordered pair written in the form (a, b).
response: Identify a as the x-coordinate (horizontal) and b as the y-coordinate (vertical).
label: Rule / Plan
example: In $(2,8)$, $2$ is the x-coordinate and $8$ is the y-coordinate.

title: Plot Ordered Pairs from Table
description: Translate paired numerical data from a table into plotted points on a coordinate grid.
condition: Given a table of corresponding values for two variables.
response: Form ordered pairs and plot each pair on the coordinate grid.
label: Production / Skill
notes: Requires coordinating table columns with x- and y-axes.

title: Plot Point from Ordered Pair
description: Plot a point on the coordinate grid by moving horizontally to the x-value and vertically to the y-value.
condition: Given an ordered pair (x, y) and a coordinate grid.
response: Move to x on the horizontal axis, then to y vertically, and mark the point.
label: Rule / Plan
example: To plot $(3,0)$, move 3 units right along the x-axis and 0 units up, marking the point at $(3,0)$.

title: Recognize Multiple Valid Classifications
description: Understand that a single shape can belong to more than one category if it satisfies multiple definitions.
condition: A shape satisfies the defining attributes of more than one category.
response: Select or state all applicable classifications.
label: Principle
example: A rectangle with opposite sides parallel is both a rectangle and a parallelogram.

title: Recognize Origin as (0,0)
description: Identify that the point where the horizontal and vertical axes intersect is the origin with coordinates (0,0).
condition: The intersection point of the x-axis and y-axis on a coordinate grid.
response: State the coordinates as (0,0).
label: Fact
example: The origin is \((0,0)\).

title: Square Is a Quadrilateral
description: Recognize that all squares belong to the broader category of quadrilaterals because they have four sides.
condition: A shape is defined as a square.
response: Conclude it is always a quadrilateral.
label: Principle
example: A square has four sides, so it is always a quadrilateral.
